Reporting of technology/cyber incidents to OSFI within 24 hours (Incident Reporting Advisory)
Effective since Jan 2024 for federally regulated financial institutions (FRFIs)
complianceProfiles.auditRetention: Technology/cyber risk and incident records retained ≥7 years to align with Canadian financial recordkeeping; logs per security monitoring program (≥1 year).
complianceProfiles.dataResidency: No absolute residency mandate, but OSFI expects FRFIs to retain control, access, and oversight; Canadian region strongly preferred for supervisory access.
howToMeet: Establish technology/cyber risk governance with documented senior accountability.
Implement secure SDLC, asset management, MFA, IAM, and privileged access controls.
Operate security monitoring/threat detection mapped to identify-defend-detect-respond-recover.
Manage cloud/third-party risk per B-10 and define tested RTO/RPO resilience objectives.
Add a 24-hour OSFI technology/cyber incident reporting runbook; prefer Canadian hosting.
complianceProfiles.regulator: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI), Canada

complianceProfiles.name: OSFI-B10
complianceProfiles.fullName: OSFI Guideline B-10: Third-Party Risk Management

forces.auth: vendor access governance, RBAC over third-party integrations
forces.data: data-handling clauses, encryption, subcontractor controls

requiredControls: Risk-based, principles-based third-party risk management lifecycle
Criticality assessment of each third-party arrangement
Contractual provisions: audit rights, sub-contracting, data location, business continuity
Ongoing monitoring of third-party performance and risk (incl. concentration risk)
Exit strategy and portability for critical arrangements
Cloud and technology service-provider oversight (links to B-13)
complianceProfiles.auditRetention: Third-party arrangement records, due diligence, and monitoring evidence retained for the life of the contract plus ≥7 years.
complianceProfiles.dataResidency: OSFI expects clarity on data location and the ability to access/audit; Canadian data location preferred for critical/material arrangements.
howToMeet: Build a third-party inventory with criticality/materiality ratings.
Embed audit rights, data-location, sub-contracting, and continuity clauses in every critical contract.
Stand up ongoing third-party monitoring including concentration-risk dashboards.
Define documented exit and portability strategies for critical providers.
Align cloud TPRM with B-13 controls and prefer Canadian data location for material arrangements.
complianceProfiles.regulator: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI), Canada

complianceProfiles.name: FINTRAC-PCMLTFA
complianceProfiles.fullName: FINTRAC obligations under the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act

forces.auth: RBAC + identity verification (KYC) controls
forces.data: KYC/beneficial-ownership records, secure long-term retention

requiredControls: Know Your Client (KYC): verify identity of clients per prescribed methods
Report Large Cash Transactions (LCTR ≥ CAD 10,000), Large Virtual Currency Transactions (LVCTR ≥ CAD 10,000), EFTs
Suspicious Transaction Reports (STR) filed as soon as practicable
Maintain a compliance program: officer, policies, risk assessment, training, biennial effectiveness review
Ongoing monitoring and record-keeping of business relationships
Travel rule for electronic funds and virtual currency transfers
complianceProfiles.auditRetention: Records (client ID, transaction, account, reports) retained 5 years from creation / end of relationship (some account records 5 years after closure) per PCMLTFA.
complianceProfiles.dataResidency: No absolute residency mandate, but records must be retrievable within 30 days for FINTRAC examination; Canadian storage strongly preferred.
howToMeet: Implement KYC identity verification and beneficial-ownership/PEP determination at onboarding.
Automate LCTR/LVCTR/EFT and STR generation and submit via FINTRAC F2R.
Maintain an immutable transaction and report audit trail with 5-year retention.
Run a documented compliance program with an officer, risk assessment, training, and biennial effectiveness review.
Ensure records are retrievable within 30 days for examination; prefer Canadian hosting.
complianceProfiles.regulator: Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada (FINTRAC)

complianceProfiles.name: SOCI
complianceProfiles.fullName: Security of Critical Infrastructure Act 2018 (Australia)

forces.auth: access governance over critical infrastructure assets
forces.data: asset register, protected information handling

requiredControls: Register of Critical Infrastructure Assets ownership/operator reporting
Critical Infrastructure Risk Management Program (CIRMP) across cyber, personnel, supply-chain, physical hazards
Mandatory cyber incident reporting to the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD/ACSC): 12 hours for critical, 72 hours for other significant incidents
Enhanced cyber security obligations for Systems of National Significance (SoNS)
Government assistance / last-resort intervention powers acknowledgment
complianceProfiles.auditRetention: CIRMP and annual attestation records retained ≥6 years (aligns with Australian corporate recordkeeping); incident records retained to evidence reporting.
complianceProfiles.dataResidency: No strict residency, but critical-asset and protected information handling is geofenced to authorized operators; sovereign-control expectations apply for SoNS.
howToMeet: Register applicable critical infrastructure assets and maintain the asset register.
Build a CIRMP covering cyber, personnel, supply-chain, and physical hazards.
Stand up incident reporting to ASD/ACSC with 12-hour (critical) / 72-hour (significant) triggers.
For SoNS, implement enhanced obligations and vulnerability assessments.
Produce an annual board-approved CIRMP attestation.
complianceProfiles.regulator: Cyber and Infrastructure Security Centre (CISC), Department of Home Affairs, Australia

complianceProfiles.name: DORA
complianceProfiles.fullName: Digital Operational Resilience Act (EU Regulation 2022/2554)

forces.auth: ICT access controls, MFA, identity management
forces.observability: ICT incident detection, classification, and reporting
forces.data: ICT risk-managed data integrity, backup, recovery
forces.network: resilience testing, segmentation, third-party ICT oversight

requiredControls: ICT risk management framework with board accountability
ICT-related incident classification and reporting to competent authorities (initial within hours, intermediate, final)
Digital operational resilience testing, incl. threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) for significant entities
ICT third-party risk management with a register of information on all ICT providers
Oversight framework for Critical ICT Third-Party Providers (CTPPs) by the ESAs
Information sharing on cyber threats among financial entities
Applies since 17 January 2025 across the EU financial sector
complianceProfiles.auditRetention: ICT incident records, resilience-testing results, and the register of information retained ≥5 years to align with EU financial recordkeeping and supervisory access.
complianceProfiles.dataResidency: No strict EU-residency mandate, but supervisory access, exit strategies, and CTPP oversight effectively constrain offshoring of critical ICT functions.
howToMeet: Implement a board-owned ICT risk management framework mapped to DORA's five pillars.
Build ICT incident classification + tiered reporting (initial/intermediate/final) to the competent authority.
Run digital operational resilience testing, including TLPT for significant entities.
Maintain the register of information on all ICT third-party providers and embed exit strategies.
Prepare for ESA oversight of Critical ICT Third-Party Providers and join threat-sharing arrangements.
complianceProfiles.regulator: European Supervisory Authorities (EBA, ESMA, EIOPA) + national competent authorities

industryRequirements.name: Financial Services
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC/OAuth2 + phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/WebAuthn) for all privileged and customer access; mTLS service-to-service; hardware-backed keys for signing
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Full immutable audit logging (WORM) with 7-year retention; SIEM ingestion (e.g. Splunk/Sentinel) with 90-day hot + 7yr cold; tamper-evident logs; transaction-level traceability; 30-min SLA on incident detection
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: AES-256 at rest + TLS 1.3 in transit; field-level encryption + tokenization for PAN/PII; HSM/KMS with BYOK; Canadian data residency (in-region) for regulated workloads; key rotation <=1yr; DLP on egress
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Dedicated regulated-tier cluster in Canadian region (e.g. ca-central-1 / Canada Central); private networking, no public ingress; SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 substrate; isolated from non-regulated tenants
keyRisks: AML/sanctions screening gaps triggering FINTRAC penalties
OSFI technology incident reporting breach (24-72hr notification)
Cross-border data transfer without adequacy / consent
industryRequirements.notes: OSFI B-13 is the anchor: report material tech/cyber incidents within 24 hours. B-10 mandates third-party risk inventory and concentration limits. FINTRAC requires STR/LCTR filing and a documented AML program. Canada-first: prefer Canadian region residency; cross-border requires PIPEDA-compliant transparency + Law 25 transfer assessment for Quebec data.

industryRequirements.name: Healthcare & Life Sciences
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC + MFA for all PHI access; role-based + attribute-based access control (least privilege); break-glass with mandatory justification logging; provincial EHR federation (e.g. SAML to provincial IAM)
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Full audit trail of every PHI access (who/what/when/why) with 10-year minimum retention; access-disclosure logging per PHIPA; anomaly detection on bulk PHI reads; consent-event logging
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: AES-256 at rest + TLS 1.3; PHI segregation; de-identification/pseudonymization for analytics; strict Canadian residency (PHIPA/provincial law often prohibits US storage); KMS with BYOK; data minimization; secure disposal
mandatoryRegimes: Provincial health privacy acts (HIA Alberta, PHIA Manitoba/NS, FOIP, BC PIPA/E-Health Act)
Quebec Law 25 + Act respecting health and social services information
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Canadian-resident, single-tenant or strongly isolated cluster with HITRUST/SOC 2 controls; provincial residency where required (e.g. BC data must stay in Canada under prior FIPPA rules, now relaxed but verify); no cross-border PHI flow without consent
keyRisks: PHI breach mandatory notification (IPC/OIPC + affected individuals)
Unauthorized secondary use of health data
Cross-border storage violating provincial residency rules
industryRequirements.notes: PHIPA governs Ontario; each province has its own health privacy statute, so residency and consent rules vary. Quebec's health information act (2023) adds strict rules. De-identification per PHIPA is the safe path for analytics. Canada-first: default to Canadian residency and provincial alignment; treat any US transfer as high-risk requiring legal review.

industryRequirements.name: Government & Public Sector
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: GC-approved IAM (e.g. credential broker / Sign-In Canada / provincial IDP) + MFA; PKI for PROTECTED data; zero-trust per ITSG-33; mandatory account recertification
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Centralized audit logging per ITSG-33 AU controls; SIEM with min 2-year retention (longer for records-management obligations); continuous monitoring; security event reporting to CCCS
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: Encryption per CSE-approved algorithms (CNSA/ITSP.40.111); Canadian residency mandatory for PROTECTED B and above; data sovereignty; CSE/CCCS cloud guardrails; key management under GC control
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: GC-assessed cloud (Protected B Medium-Integrity-Medium-Availability) in Canadian region; FedRAMP/CCCS-assessed; dedicated landing zone with GC guardrails; segregated from commercial tenants
keyRisks: PROTECTED data residency or sovereignty violation
Inadequate ITSG-33 control implementation blocking ATO
Citizen PII breach and Privacy Commissioner findings
industryRequirements.notes: ITSG-33 is the controls catalogue (NIST 800-53 aligned). PROTECTED B is the common bar for citizen data and requires Canadian residency + CCCS-assessed cloud. TBS Directive on Service and Digital mandates cloud-first with guardrails. Canada-first by definition; sovereignty is non-negotiable for PB+.

industryRequirements.name: Defense & Intelligence
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: PKI / smart-card (DND PKI) + hardware MFA; air-gapped or cross-domain-solution-mediated access for classified; strict need-to-know with compartmentalization; continuous adjudication of clearances
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Full audit on all classified-system access with indefinite/long retention; cross-domain transfer logging; insider-threat monitoring (UAM); air-gap integrity monitoring; CCCS incident reporting
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: CSE-approved cryptography (CNSA 2.0 / post-quantum migration in progress); strict Canadian sovereignty; classified data never on commercial cloud without accreditation; CDS for any cross-domain flow; controlled goods handling
mandatoryRegimes: Canadian Industrial Security Directorate (CISD/Contract Security Program)
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Accredited classified enclave (air-gapped or CDS-mediated); DND/CAF-approved on-prem or sovereign cloud; no commercial multi-tenant; physically and logically isolated; TEMPEST considerations for SECRET+
keyRisks: Spillage of classified data to lower-classification networks
industryRequirements.notes: This vertical is sovereignty-absolute: classified workloads do not run on commercial cloud without formal accreditation. Controlled Goods Program governs physical/logical access to controlled tech. ITAR applies to US-origin components and restricts foreign-person access. CNSA 2.0 / PQC migration is active in 2026. Canada-first and Canada-only for classified.

industryRequirements.name: Energy & Environment
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC + MFA for IT; strict IT/OT separation; jump-host + privileged access management for OT/ICS; no shared credentials on control systems; vendor remote access brokered and recorded
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Full audit on control systems and IT; OT network monitoring (e.g. Claroty/Dragos); 3-year+ retention for critical infrastructure; CER/NERC CIP event logging; mandatory cyber incident reporting under critical-infra rules
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: Encryption at rest + in transit for IT/business data; OT segmentation (Purdue model / IEC 62443 zones); SCADA data integrity; Canadian residency for critical-infrastructure operational data; secure historian
mandatoryRegimes: Critical Cyber Systems Protection Act (CCSPA / Bill C-26)
Provincial energy regulators (e.g. OEB, AUC, BCUC)
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Segregated IT cluster in Canadian region for business workloads; OT kept on isolated, air-gapped-leaning networks; CCSPA-designated operators need documented cyber security program; SOC 2 substrate
keyRisks: NERC CIP violation with monetary penalties
CCSPA non-compliance once in force (designated critical operators)
industryRequirements.notes: Bill C-26 / CCSPA designates critical cyber systems (energy, telecom, finance, transport) and mandates a cyber security program plus incident reporting to CCCS. NERC CIP applies to the bulk electric system spanning the Canada-US grid. IEC 62443 is the OT security standard. Canada-first: critical-infra operational data should stay in Canada.

industryRequirements.name: Technology / SaaS
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC/OAuth2 + MFA (TOTP min, WebAuthn preferred); SSO with SCIM provisioning; short-lived tokens; secrets in vault (not env); least-privilege IAM with regular access reviews
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Centralized structured logging + distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry); SIEM/observability stack (e.g. Datadog/Grafana); 1-year audit log retention minimum; SLO-based alerting; security event monitoring
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: TLS 1.3 + AES-256; tenant isolation (logical or physical); customer-managed keys option (BYOK); data residency choice incl Canadian region; secrets rotation; DLP for source/IP
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Multi-region Kubernetes with Canadian-region option for Canada-first customers; SOC 2 + ISO 27001 controls; logical multi-tenancy with hardened isolation; offer single-tenant for regulated buyers
keyRisks: Account takeover via weak SSO config
industryRequirements.notes: SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 are de-facto entry requirements to sell B2B in Canada. Law 25 applies to any org handling Quebec residents' data and adds breach reporting, privacy-by-default, and transfer assessments. CASL is uniquely Canadian and strict on consent. Offer Canadian residency to win regulated customers.

industryRequirements.name: Telecommunications
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC + MFA for subscriber-data and network-management access; PAM for network elements; lawful-intercept systems on isolated, tightly-controlled access; SIM/eSIM provisioning security
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: CDR and network event logging; subscriber-access audit with multi-year retention; SIEM with OT/network monitoring; CCSPA incident reporting; lawful-intercept audit segregation
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: Encryption of subscriber PII + traffic metadata; CPNI-equivalent confidentiality; Canadian residency for subscriber data; secure storage of lawful-intercept data; network segmentation (5G core isolation)
mandatoryRegimes: Critical Cyber Systems Protection Act (CCSPA / Bill C-26 — telecom is designated)
CCCS / CSE security guidance (incl Huawei/ZTE 5G restrictions)
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Carrier-grade segregated infrastructure; subscriber data in Canadian region; 5G core on isolated network slices; CCSPA cyber security program; supplier restrictions on high-risk vendors
keyRisks: CCSPA non-compliance (telecom is a designated sector)
industryRequirements.notes: Telecom is explicitly named under Bill C-26 / CCSPA as a critical cyber system; designated operators must run a documented cyber security program and report incidents to CCCS. Canada banned Huawei/ZTE from 5G/4G networks. CRTC governs service obligations. Canada-first: subscriber data resides in Canada.

industryRequirements.name: Retail & Commerce
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC/OAuth2 + MFA for admin/back-office; customer auth with passwordless/MFA option; PCI-scoped systems behind strict access controls; POS terminal hardening
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Transaction + access audit logging; PCI-DSS log retention (min 1 year, 3 months immediately available); fraud monitoring; cardholder-data-environment (CDE) logging segregation; SIEM
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: PAN tokenization + AES-256; PCI-DSS scope minimization (never store CVV); TLS 1.3 at checkout; PII encryption; Canadian residency option for customer data; consent management for marketing
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: PCI-scoped CDE isolated from general workloads; Canadian-region option for customer PII; tokenization service; SOC 2 substrate; CDN/WAF at edge for bot/fraud defense
keyRisks: PCI-DSS v4 non-compliance after 2025 mandatory date
industryRequirements.notes: PCI-DSS v4.0.1 future-dated requirements became mandatory March 31, 2025 (incl client-side script monitoring for skimming). Tokenize PAN to shrink scope. CASL governs all commercial electronic messages incl loyalty. Canada-first: offer Canadian residency for customer PII and keep Interac flows compliant.

industryRequirements.name: Manufacturing & Industrial
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC + MFA for IT/ERP/MES; strict IT/OT separation; PAM and brokered remote access for plant-floor/OT; no default vendor credentials on PLCs; jump hosts for maintenance
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: IT audit logging + OT network monitoring (Dragos/Claroty/Nozomi); 1-3 year retention; production-line event logging; supply-chain traceability; anomaly detection on OT protocols (Modbus/OPC-UA)
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: Encryption of IP/CAD/PLM data; OT segmentation per IEC 62443 / Purdue; secure historian; IP exfiltration DLP; Canadian residency for sensitive trade-secret data; backup integrity
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Segregated IT cluster for ERP/MES in Canadian region; OT on isolated networks with one-way data diodes to historian; SOC 2 substrate; IP-sensitive workloads single-tenant
keyRisks: Supply-chain compromise via vendor remote access
Legacy PLC vulnerabilities with no patch path
industryRequirements.notes: IEC 62443 is the governing OT security framework; segment using zones/conduits and the Purdue model. Manufacturers handling dual-use or defense components fall under Controlled Goods and possibly ITAR. IP theft is the dominant data risk. Canada-first: keep trade-secret data resident in Canada and isolate OT.

industryRequirements.name: Media & Entertainment
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC + MFA for production/asset systems; DRM-tied entitlement auth for distribution; vendor/freelancer access time-boxed; MFA on MAM/DAM and editorial CMS
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Asset-access and content-movement audit logging; watermarking/forensic tracing on pre-release; 1-year+ retention; piracy/leak detection; CDN access logs
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: Encryption of master/pre-release assets; DRM (Widevine/PlayReady/FairPlay) for distribution; forensic watermarking; access-controlled MAM; Canadian residency option for subscriber data; geo-fencing rights
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Content-secure cluster aligned to TPN (Trusted Partner Network) controls; pre-release assets isolated and watermarked; Canadian-region option for subscriber PII; CDN with token-auth and DRM at edge
industryRequirements.notes: Content security follows MPA/TPN best practices (least the studios accept). Bill C-11 (Online Streaming Act) brings streamers under CRTC with CanCon contribution obligations in 2026. Forensic watermarking + DRM are the core technical controls. Canada-first: subscriber data residency and CanCon compliance.

industryRequirements.name: Education
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC/SAML + MFA for staff/faculty; SSO federation (e.g. provincial/institutional IDP); student auth with MFA option; parental-consent flows for minors; LMS access controls
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Access audit on student records (SIS/LMS); 1-3 year retention; FOIP/FIPPA disclosure logging; anomaly detection on bulk record access; consent-event logging for minors
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: Encryption of student PII/records; data minimization; Canadian residency (many provincial K-12/post-secondary rules and FIPPA require it); de-identification for analytics; restricted EdTech vendor data sharing
mandatoryRegimes: Provincial FOIP/FIPPA (public institutions, e.g. BC, Alberta, Ontario FIPPA/MFIPPA)
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Canadian-resident cluster for student records; FIPPA-aligned where the institution is public; SOC 2 substrate; isolated EdTech integrations with vetted data-sharing agreements
keyRisks: EdTech vendor over-collection and secondary use
Cross-border storage violating provincial FIPPA residency
Minor data handling without proper consent
industryRequirements.notes: Public institutions are governed by provincial FOIP/FIPPA which historically restricted personal data storage to Canada (BC/NS relaxed but verify per province). Quebec Law 25 applies to education bodies. EdTech vendor governance is the recurring weak point. Canada-first: default to Canadian residency for student records.

industryRequirements.name: Transportation & Logistics
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC + MFA for TMS/WMS and operations; PAM for fleet/telematics and signaling systems; driver/operator credential management; vendor access brokered for connected vehicles
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Shipment + access audit logging; telematics event logging; 1-3 year retention; OT monitoring for rail/port control systems; CCSPA incident reporting (transport is designated); chain-of-custody traceability
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: Encryption of shipment, customs, and PII data; telematics/location data protection; Canadian residency for critical-infra operational data; secure EDI/API; customs data integrity
mandatoryRegimes: Critical Cyber Systems Protection Act (CCSPA / Bill C-26 — transport designated)
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Segregated IT cluster in Canadian region for ops/customer data; OT (rail/port/ATC-adjacent) isolated; CCSPA cyber security program for designated operators; SOC 2 substrate
keyRisks: OT compromise of rail/port/fleet control systems
CCSPA non-compliance (transport is a designated sector)
industryRequirements.notes: Transportation is a designated critical sector under Bill C-26 / CCSPA — designated operators must maintain a cyber security program and report incidents. Transport Canada governs TDG and modal security. CBSA ACI mandates advance electronic cargo data. Canada-first: keep critical-infra operational data resident in Canada.

industryRequirements.name: Real Estate & Construction
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC + MFA for transaction/escrow and document systems; role-based access to deal data; MFA on wire/trust-account approvals; vendor access scoped for BIM/project portals
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Audit logging on transactions, trust accounts, and document access; 7-year retention for financial/trust records; wire-fraud anomaly detection; access logs on sensitive deal rooms
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: Encryption of PII, financial, and deal data; trust-account segregation; document integrity (e-signature audit); Canadian residency for client PII; secure BIM/CAD storage; DLP on deal exfiltration
mandatoryRegimes: FINTRAC / PCMLTFA (real estate is a reporting sector for AML)
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Canadian-region cluster for client PII and financial data; SOC 2 substrate; isolated deal-room/document store with strong access control; trust-account systems segregated
keyRisks: FINTRAC AML obligations missed (real estate is a reporting entity)
industryRequirements.notes: Real estate brokers and developers are FINTRAC reporting entities under PCMLTFA — must conduct client ID verification, keep records, and file STRs. Business-email-compromise wire fraud is the dominant active threat. Canada-first: keep client PII and trust-account data resident in Canada; align to provincial real estate regulators.

industryRequirements.name: Agriculture / AgTech
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC + MFA for farm-management and supply-chain platforms; device auth for IoT/precision-ag sensors; PAM for equipment telematics; scoped grower/vendor access
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Audit logging on farm data and traceability events; 1-3 year retention; IoT device telemetry monitoring; food-traceability event logging (lot/batch); anomaly detection on data feeds
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: Encryption of farm/agronomic and PII data; grower data-ownership controls; Canadian residency option (food-security sensitivity); IoT data integrity; secure equipment OTA; traceability data immutability
mandatoryRegimes: Safe Food for Canadians Act/Regulations (SFCR — CFIA traceability)
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Canadian-region cluster for grower data and traceability; SOC 2 substrate; IoT ingest isolated with device identity; immutable traceability ledger; offer grower data-ownership guarantees
industryRequirements.notes: SFCR (Safe Food for Canadians Regulations) mandates traceability one-step-forward/one-step-back and recall capability enforced by CFIA. Grower data ownership and sovereignty is an emerging trust issue in AgTech. Canada-first: keep grower data and traceability records resident in Canada given food-security sensitivity.

industryRequirements.name: Legal & Professional Services
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC + MFA for all matter/client access; strict ethical-wall (information-barrier) access controls; PAM for document management; client-matter scoped permissions; MFA on trust-account approvals
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Audit logging on all client/matter document access; 7-10 year retention (limitation + records rules); ethical-wall breach detection; trust-account audit trail; anomaly detection on bulk document export
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: Encryption of privileged/client data; solicitor-client privilege protection; information barriers enforced technically; Canadian residency strongly preferred (privilege + law-society guidance); DLP on document exfiltration; secure disposal
mandatoryRegimes: FINTRAC / PCMLTFA (legal counsel — note SCC carve-out, but law-society AML rules apply)
Law Society rules (per province — confidentiality, trust accounting)
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Canadian-region cluster for privileged data; SOC 2 + ISO 27001 substrate; single-tenant or strongly isolated; technical ethical walls; vendor with law-society-aligned cloud guidance
keyRisks: Cross-border storage waiving or weakening privilege
AML obligations under law-society client-ID rules
industryRequirements.notes: FINTRAC's direct application to legal counsel was struck by the SCC (2015), but provincial law societies impose equivalent client-ID and trust-accounting AML rules. Solicitor-client privilege favors Canadian residency to avoid foreign-disclosure exposure. Law-society cloud guidance (e.g. LSO/LSBC) is the operative standard. Canada-first.

industryRequirements.name: Non-Profit & Social Sector
industryRequirements.requiredAuth: OIDC/OAuth2 + MFA for staff and donor/case-management systems; role-based access to beneficiary data; MFA on financial/grant systems; volunteer access scoped and time-boxed
industryRequirements.requiredObservability: Audit logging on donor and beneficiary records; 7-year retention for charitable financial records (CRA); access logging on sensitive case files; anomaly detection on donor-data export
industryRequirements.requiredDataControls: Encryption of donor PII, payment, and beneficiary data; vulnerable-population data minimization; Canadian residency option; PCI scope for donations; consent management; secure disposal of case records
mandatoryRegimes: CRA charitable registration + Income Tax Act reporting
industryRequirements.recommendedCluster: Canadian-region cluster for donor and beneficiary data; SOC 2 substrate (or cost-appropriate equivalent); PCI-scoped donation path isolated; strong access control on vulnerable-population records
keyRisks: CRA compliance / charitable-status risk on financial records
industryRequirements.notes: Charities are PIPEDA-covered for commercial activities and must keep CRA-mandated financial records (typically 6 years after the tax year, longer for some). Beneficiary data for vulnerable populations demands extra minimization and access control. CASL applies to fundraising. Canada-first: keep donor and beneficiary data resident in Canada; isolate PCI donation flows.

attrs.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
attrs.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push
attrs.phase: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)
Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

attrs.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
attrs.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
attrs.phase: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

attrs.command: Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all

envInstructions.text: SCA note: Replace npm with yarn audit or pnpm audit per ARG PM
envInstructions.label: SCA note:
envInstructions.instruction: Replace npm with yarn audit or pnpm audit per ARG PM

envInstructions.text: Build note: ARG PM=pnpm → pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm run build
envInstructions.label: Build note:
envInstructions.instruction: ARG PM=pnpm → pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm run build

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch

stages.tool: ESLint + Prettier + Error Lens (VS Code / WebStorm)

stages.command: docker build --platform linux/amd64 --target runtime -t app:pr-${{ github.sha }} --load .
semantic-release --no-ci → applies semver tag to image
Push completed in Phase 3 Build + Push above

phases.name: Promotions — DEV → TEST → STAGING → PROD (same pattern for all frameworks)

stages.command: Sync from dev overlay; wait for rollout
Run integration test suite against deployed service
Required approvers listed in environment settings
Auto-rollback if error rate >1% or p99 >2s

envInstructions.text: SCA note: Replace npm with yarn audit or pnpm audit per ARG PM
envInstructions.label: SCA note:
envInstructions.instruction: Replace npm with yarn audit or pnpm audit per ARG PM

envInstructions.text: Build note: Output served by nginx — no Node.js at runtime
envInstructions.label: Build note:
envInstructions.instruction: Output served by nginx — no Node.js at runtime

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.name: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.name: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

stages.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
stages.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push

stages.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch

stages.tool: ESLint + Prettier + Error Lens (VS Code / WebStorm)

stages.command: docker build --platform linux/amd64 --target runtime -t app:pr-${{ github.sha }} --load .
semantic-release --no-ci → applies semver tag to image
Push completed in Phase 3 Build + Push above

phases.name: Promotions — DEV → TEST → STAGING → PROD (same pattern for all frameworks)

stages.command: Sync from dev overlay; wait for rollout
Run integration test suite against deployed service
Required approvers listed in environment settings
Auto-rollback if error rate >1% or p99 >2s

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period

phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced

phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings

envInstructions.instruction: Output served by nginx — no Node.js at runtime

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.name: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

stages.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
stages.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push

stages.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch

stages.tool: VS Code + React Native Tools + Flipper

stages.command: npm test && npx detox test --configuration android.emu.release

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code

phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.Phase 0: Confirm registry supports signed FIPS images
phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths

phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production

phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

phases.Phase 2: Build with --target runtime-fips in FIPS-capable builder
phases.Phase 3: Push both runtime and runtime-fips tags; sign both with cosign
phases.Registry: Store both :$SHA and :$SHA-fips tags; sign-verify both
phases.Promotions: Deploy runtime-fips image to all environments; enforce in Kyverno policy

phases.Phase 0: Restrict registry write to release branch only; enable audit logging
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE (Trivy --exit-code 1 --severity CRITICAL); fail on hardcoded secrets
phases.Phase 3: Require zero CRITICAL CVEs before sign; add payment-specific SAST rules
phases.Registry: Enable immutable tags; log all pull events; restrict pull to production namespaces
phases.Promotions: Network segmentation policy in Kyverno; no internet egress from payment containers

phases.Phase 0: Enable audit logging on registry; document BAA with cloud provider
phases.Phase 2: Fail on HIGH+CRITICAL CVEs; scan for PHI patterns in source code
phases.Phase 3: Enforce TLS 1.2+ in app config; non-root USER; read-only filesystem where possible
phases.Registry: Enable access logging; restrict pull to HIPAA-scoped namespaces
phases.Promotions: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted); seccompProfile RuntimeDefault

complianceDeltas.appliesTo: US federal agencies and SaaS providers

phases.Phase 0: OIDC trust to FedRAMP-authorized cloud region; FIPS registry access only
phases.Phase 2: FIPS build target required; SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format; SLSA Level 3 attestation
phases.Phase 3: Container must use FIPS-certified runtime; cosign sign with hardware key or Fulcio
phases.Registry: Deploy to GovCloud (AWS us-gov-* / Azure USGov); immutable tags enforced
phases.Promotions: Continuous monitoring via AWS Config / Azure Policy; runtime image scanned daily

phases.Phase 0: MFA on all CI/CD accounts; OIDC for keyless auth; CODEOWNERS for all paths
phases.Phase 2: Access control: only named reviewers can approve PRs containing CUI
phases.Phase 3: Full audit trail: every pipeline run logged, signed, and retained 3 years
phases.Registry: Restrict to US-only regions; no cross-region replication to foreign jurisdictions
phases.Promotions: Incident response plan documented; deployment events logged to SIEM

phases.Phase 0: Enable branch protection evidence collection; log all admin actions
phases.Phase 2: Evidence: save scan results as artifacts for audit evidence packages
phases.Phase 3: Retain all pipeline artifacts (logs, SBOMs, scan results) for audit period
phases.Registry: Enable pull logging; image retention policy documented
phases.Promotions: Change management: every deployment linked to approved ticket; rollback tested

phases.Phase 0: Segregation of duties: developer cannot approve own PR or deploy to production
phases.Phase 2: Required reviewer must be from a separate team (enforce via CODEOWNERS)
phases.Phase 3: Change management ticket required before merge to main; approval documented
phases.Registry: Immutable image tags; no manual overwrites in production registry
phases.Promotions: PROD deployment requires second-person authorization; audit log exported to GRC tool

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in EU regions (GitHub Actions self-hosted or EU data center)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for hardcoded PII; fail on any PII found in code or config
phases.Phase 3: Log processing activities in image labels; ensure encryption at rest enforced
phases.Registry: Registry must be in EU region; cross-border transfer documented
phases.Promotions: Data residency enforced in Kubernetes namespace policy; egress to non-EU blocked

phases.Phase 0: Confirm all CI/CD infra in Canadian regions (AWS ca-central-1 / Azure Canada Central)
phases.Phase 2: Scan source for Canadian PII patterns; fail on any found
phases.Phase 3: Ensure image metadata documents data processing purpose
phases.Registry: Registry must be in Canadian region; no personal data in image layers
phases.Promotions: Namespace policy enforces ca-central-1 / Canada Central regions only

phases.Phase 0: Air-gap CI from internet where possible; strict access control with role-based auth
phases.Phase 2: Fail on any CRITICAL CVE; no internet access during build (vendored deps)
phases.Phase 3: Offline build environment; all deps vendored; no external network calls in build
phases.Registry: Air-gapped private registry; no public registry pull
phases.Promotions: Change control ticket with CIP change management evidence; rollback plan documented

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.name: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

stages.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
stages.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push

stages.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch

stages.tool: Intelephense + PHP_CodeSniffer + VS Code PHP extension

stages.command: docker build --platform linux/amd64 --target runtime -t app:pr-${{ github.sha }} --load .
semantic-release --no-ci → applies semver tag to image
Push completed in Phase 3 Build + Push above

phases.name: Promotions — DEV → TEST → STAGING → PROD (same pattern for all frameworks)

stages.command: Sync from dev overlay; wait for rollout
Run integration test suite against deployed service
Required approvers listed in environment settings
Auto-rollback if error rate >1% or p99 >2s

phases.Phase 0: ISMS asset register: document all pipeline components and their risk ratings
phases.Phase 2: Risk-based scan thresholds: document risk acceptance for deferred vulnerabilities
phases.Phase 3: Full artifact retention per retention schedule (usually 3–5 years)
phases.Registry: Access review quarterly; registry credentials rotated annually
phases.Promotions: Incident response runbook linked from deployment pipeline; change approval documented

phases.name: Phase 0 — Bootstrap (once, same for all frameworks — see global section below)

stages.tool: GitHub repo settings / GitLab protected branches
stages.command: Protect main; require PR; enforce status checks; no direct push

stages.tool: GitHub OIDC + cloud IAM / Workload Identity
stages.command: Add permissions: id-token: write to all CI workflows; no stored cloud credentials
Scope push permission to exact repo + branch only; pull allowed from all
Block unsigned or unscanned images cluster-wide; enforce runAsNonRoot
Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; weekly updates; auto-merge patch
docker build --platform linux/amd64 --target runtime -t app:pr-${{ github.sha }} --load .
semantic-release --no-ci → applies semver tag to image
Push completed in Phase 3 Build + Push above

phases.name: Promotions — DEV → TEST → STAGING → PROD (same pattern for all frameworks)

stages.command: Sync from dev overlay; wait for rollout
Run integration test suite against deployed service
Required approvers listed in environment settings
Auto-rollback if error rate >1% or p99 >2s

envInstructions.instruction: Also run: swift package show-dependencies | grep -i cve
